title: Changelog / release feed
description: Data contract for the changelog format: a reverse-chronological release feed. Each version is a semver tag + date + optional channel, holding typed change items (added/changed/fixed/deprecated/removed/security/breaking) rendered as coloured chips, with a sticky left version jump rail, type/channel filters, a breaking-only toggle, and live highlight search. The #report-data instance in changelog_sample.html is the canonical example. Default theme: slate. Order versions newest-first.

meta.description: Shared report identity block (see _DATA_DRIVEN.md). Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

org.description: Organisation wordmark shown in header + footer.

project.description: Project / system this changelog belongs to.

reportType.description: Artefact type label shown in the eyebrow + footer.

title.description: Changelog title (the heading above the feed).

subtitle.description: Optional one-line context shown under the title.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; set on <html data-theme>. Live-switchable via .rc-theme. Default slate for this format.

product.description: The thing being versioned — shown beside the title in the header.

name.description: Product / service / library name being versioned.

repoUrl.description: Optional source/repo URL; rendered as a header reference chip. Must be a same-document-safe link (no external asset fetch).

versions.description: Released versions, ordered NEWEST-FIRST (reverse-chronological). Each becomes a collapsible feed block and a version-rail entry. The renderer derives all counts from this array; do not hard-code totals.

version.description: Semver-style version tag (the .cl-tag pill). Used to build the rail deep-link id.

channel.description: Release channel; drives the channel badge and the channel filter. 'stable' is neutral; 'beta' is hold-tinted. Defaults to 'stable' when omitted.

summary.description: Optional one-line release summary shown in the version header.

yanked.description: If true, this release was withdrawn/yanked; rendered with a risk 'Yanked' marker and strike treatment.

changes.description: The typed change items for this version. Each carries a type (drives the coloured chip) and terse one-line text, with an optional reference.

type.description: Kind of change → the coloured chip. 'breaking' uses the risk (bordeaux) tone; 'deprecated'/'removed' use hold (amber); 'added'/'changed'/'fixed'/'security' use the brand accent / ok shades. No type outside this set.

text.description: The change description — one terse line. Inline `code` (single backticks) is allowed and rendered as <code>; everything else is escaped.

ref.description: Optional reference label (PR / issue / ticket / commit), rendered as a .ref-chip.

refUrl.description: Optional URL the ref chip links to. Use '#' or a safe placeholder for brand-neutral samples (no external asset fetch).

meta.title: Payments API — Release changelog
meta.subtitle: Every shipped version of the Payments API, newest first. Filter by change type or channel, search, or jump to a version from the rail. Breaking changes are flagged in bordeaux.
meta.author: Research Desk

versions.summary: Idempotency hardening and the Node 18 runtime drop.

changes.text: Drop support for Node 18 runtimes; minimum supported runtime is now Node 20. Upgrade your workers before deploying.
New `Idempotency-Key` request header on `POST /v4/payments` — retries with the same key are now safely deduplicated server-side.
Default request timeout raised from `10s` to `30s` for the settlement endpoints to absorb downstream latency spikes.
Retry storm when the rate limiter returned `429` without a `Retry-After` header; the client now backs off exponentially.

versions.summary: Security patch plus webhook delivery improvements.

changes.text: Patch a token-leak where bearer tokens could appear in debug-level request logs (CVE-2026-31188).
Webhook deliveries now include an `X-Signature-256` HMAC header for payload verification.
Pagination cursor occasionally skipped the last page when `limit` exactly divided the result count.

versions.summary: Major release — v3 sunset and the new resource layout.

changes.text: Remove the legacy `/v3/*` endpoints; all traffic must move to `/v4/*`. See the migration guide.
`amount` fields are now integer minor units (e.g. cents) instead of decimal strings.
Remove the deprecated `GET /v3/balance` endpoint (superseded by `GET /v4/accounts/{id}/balance`).
Error envelope reshaped to `{ error: { code, message, details } }` across all endpoints.

versions.summary: Second beta of the v4 line — feedback wanted before GA.

changes.text: Preview of the bulk `POST /v4/payments:batch` endpoint (up to 500 items per request).
Beta-only: rename the `idempotency_token` field to `idempotency_key` to match the GA naming.
Batch endpoint returned `200` on partial failures; it now returns `207 Multi-Status`.

versions.summary: Deprecation notices ahead of the v4 cutover.

changes.text: Deprecate the `/v3/*` endpoints; they will be removed in `4.0.0`. Plan your migration now.
Deprecate decimal-string `amount` values; integer minor units arrive in `4.0.0`.
Add `X-Request-Id` to every response to make support tickets traceable.
Idempotency cache evicted keys 60s early under sustained load.
Refund webhooks fired twice when the gateway acknowledged slowly.
Bump the JSON parser dependency to close a denial-of-service via deeply nested payloads.
